title: How do I block an initial search using onStateChange?
body:
### 🐛 Current behavior

On the docs for the [`searchFunction`](https://www.algolia.com/doc/api-reference/widgets/instantsearch/js/#widget-param-searchfunction) parameter, it says it can:
>avoid doing searches at page load

It also says that `searchFunction` is _deprecated_ and to use [`onStateChange`](https://www.algolia.com/doc/api-reference/widgets/instantsearch/js/#widget-param-onstatechange) instead.

The problem is, nowhere in the docs does it explain _how_ to use `onStateChange` to "avoid doing searches at page load."  I cannot figure out how this can be done.  This seems similar to #6194.

When I've been testing, it seems that `onStateChange` doesn't run on the initial page load, so I'm completely lost on how to use it instead of `searchFunction`.

### 🔍 Steps to reproduce

1. Use `searchFunction` to block "searches at page load" (as shown in the documentation).
2. Get a warning that `searchFunction` is deprecated.
3. Remove `searchFunction` and add the following code
    ```
    const search = instantsearch({
        onStateChange({ uiState, setUiState }) {
            console.log(uiState);
            setUiState(uiState);
        },
    });
    ```
4. The `console.log` is not ran on initial page load.

### Live reproduction

https://codesandbox.io/p/devbox/condescending-ioana-g8xm3n?workspaceId=ws_VqZ4hPZBPfmrwnc7dbPq5F

### 💭 Expected behavior

I never see the `console.log(uiState);` being ran, so I have no idea how I'm supposed to use `onStateChange` to "avoid doing searches at page load" since `searchFunction` is deprecated.

### Package version

instantsearch.js 4.77.3

### Operating system

_No response_

### Browser

_No response_
